depression.description: I often feel like I'm unlovable and that nobody truly cares about me. It makes me believe that I will always be alone, especially during times when I need connection the most.
I keep thinking about how I've always given to others without receiving anything in return. This makes me feel sad and resentful, especially when I reflect on the holidays and my isolation.

depression.topic: Struggling with motivation to reach out
depression.description: I want to reconnect with people, but I hesitate because I'm afraid they will just hurt me again. It feels pointless to try when I believe it will only lead to disappointment.
I find myself isolating at home, often just staying in my bedroom. It's my way of avoiding the overwhelming feelings of rage and sadness that come from interacting with others.

Delusion.topic: Belief that people take advantage of me
Delusion.description: I feel like everyone around me is just waiting to take advantage of my kindness. This belief makes it hard for me to trust anyone.
When I think about reaching out to someone who has hurt me, I can't shake the feeling that it will just lead to the same cycle of pain and disappointment. It's easier to stay away.
I feel a constant need to protect myself from being hurt by others. This leads me to avoid social situations altogether, which only deepens my loneliness.
